topic.what: First clinical description linking chronic atrophic gastritis to pernicious anemia via progressive gastric gland destruction.
topic.how: Clinical-pathological correlation from case series observations of patients with chronic stomach disease and concurrent anemia.
topic.why_matters: Identified one of the earliest known autoimmune disease pathways — decades before autoimmunity was conceptualized — establishing atrophic gastritis as a systemic disease, not merely a local stomach condition.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether this was the first description of the phenomenon
characteristics.value: First to link atrophic gastritis to pernicious anemia
characteristics.vs_baseline: No prior description of this association

novelty: First identification of gastric gland destruction as a progressive pathological process
First clinical association between stomach atrophy and systemic anemia
Anticipated autoimmune gastritis concept by nearly a century
open_problems: B12 not yet identified as the missing factor
Small case series by modern standards
